server.h(3) Library Functions Manual server.h(3)

NAME

server.h - WebSocket server for accepting browser client connections.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct websocket_client_context_t
Context passed to client handler thread. struct websocket_server_config_t
WebSocket server configuration. struct websocket_server
WebSocket server state.

Typedefs


typedef struct websocket_server websocket_server_t
typedef struct thread_pool thread_pool_t
typedef void *(* websocket_client_handler_fn) (void *arg)
Client handler function type.

Functions


asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t *server, const websocket_server_config_t *config)
Initialize WebSocket server. asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t *server)
Run WebSocket server event loop. void websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t *server)
Destroy WebSocket server and free resources. void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t *server)
Wake the WebSocket server event loop immediately.

Detailed Description

WebSocket server for accepting browser client connections.

Provides a WebSocket server implementation using libwebsockets to accept connections from browser-based WASM clients alongside the TCP server.

This module mirrors the tcp_server API to provide consistent server-side connection handling regardless of transport type.

Usage Pattern

1.
Configure server with websocket_server_config_t
2.
Call websocket_server_init() to create libwebsockets context
3.
Call websocket_server_run() to start event loop (blocks)
4.
Signal shutdown by setting running flag to false
5.
Call websocket_server_destroy() to clean up

Example

// Define client handler (same as TCP)
void *my_client_handler(void *arg) {


    websocket_client_context_t *ctx = (websocket_client_context_t *)arg;


    // Process client connection via transport


    acip_transport_destroy(ctx->transport);


    SAFE_FREE(ctx);


    return NULL;
}
// Configure and run server
websocket_server_config_t config = {


    .port = 27224,


    .client_handler = my_client_handler,


    .user_data = NULL
};
websocket_server_t server;
if (websocket_server_init(&server, &config) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {


    websocket_server_run(&server);


    websocket_server_destroy(&server);
}

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct thread_pool thread_pool_t

Definition at line 63 of file include/ascii-chat/network/websocket/server.h.

typedef void *(* websocket_client_handler_fn) (void *arg)

Client handler function type. Called when a new WebSocket client connects. The handler receives a fully initialized transport ready for ACIP packets.

Parameters

arg websocket_client_context_t* containing connection info

Returns

Thread exit value (ignored)

Definition at line 74 of file include/ascii-chat/network/websocket/server.h.

typedef struct websocket_server websocket_server_t

Definition at line 61 of file include/ascii-chat/network/websocket/server.h.

Function Documentation

void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t * server)

Wake the WebSocket server event loop immediately. Thread-safe. Wakes lws_service() from its timeout wait, allowing the event loop to check the running flag without waiting for the full service timeout (50ms).

Parameters

server Server instance (must have a valid context)

Definition at line 850 of file lib/network/websocket/server.c.

850                                                                  {
851   if (server && server->context) {
852     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
853   }
854 }




References websocket_server::context.


Referenced by server_main().






void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Destroy WebSocket server and free resources.


Parameters


server Server instance to destroy


Definition at line 856 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


856                                                           {
857   if (!server) {
858     return;
859   }
860 
861   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
862 
863   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
864   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
865   if (server->context) {
866     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
867     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
868   }
869 
870   // Unregister atomic fields
871   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
872 
873   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
874   if (server->handler_pool) {
875     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
876     server->handler_pool = NULL;
877   }
878 
879   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
880   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
881   // or didn't complete normally.
882   if (server->context) {
883     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
884     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
885     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
886     server->context = NULL;
887   }
888 
889   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
890 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, NAMED_UNREGISTER, websocket_server::running,
    and thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init
  (websocket_server_t * server, const websocket_server_config_t *
  config)


Initialize WebSocket server. Creates libwebsockets context and
    prepares for accepting connections.




Parameters


server Server instance to initialize


Config Module Server configuration


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 688 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


688                                                                                                              {
689   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
690     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
691   }
692 
693   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
694   server->handler = config->client_handler;
695   server->user_data = config->user_data;
696   server->port = config->port;
697   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
698 
699   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
700   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
701   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
702 
703   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
704   lws_log_init_server();
705 
706   // HTTP mount for /health endpoint (required for LWS_CALLBACK_HTTP to fire)
707   static const struct lws_http_mount health_mount = {
708       .mount_next = NULL,
709       .mountpoint = "/",
710       .mountpoint_len = 1,
711       .origin = "http",
712       .def = NULL,
713       .protocol = "http",
714       .origin_protocol = LWSMPRO_CALLBACK,
715   };
716 
717   // Configure libwebsockets context
718   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
719   info.port = config->port;
720   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
721   info.mounts = &health_mount;
722   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
723   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
724   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
725   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
726   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
727 
728   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
729   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
730       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
731 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
732     // Validate certificate and key files are readable
733     if (access(config->tls_cert_path, R_OK) != 0) {
734       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS certificate file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_cert_path,
735                        errno);
736     }
737     if (access(config->tls_key_path, R_OK) != 0) {
738       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "TLS key file not readable: %s (errno=%d)", config->tls_key_path, errno);
739     }
740 
741     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
742     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
743     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
744 #else
745     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
746 #endif
747   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
748              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
749     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
750   }
751 
752   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
753   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
754   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
755   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
756 
757   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
758   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
759   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
760   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
761   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
762   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
763 
764   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
765   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
766   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
767   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
768 
769   // Create libwebsockets context
770   // Log diagnostic info before attempting creation
771   log_debug("lws_create_context: port=%u, protocols=%p, options=0x%x", info.port, (void *)info.protocols, info.options);
772 
773   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
774   if (!server->context) {
775     // Capture any OpenSSL errors that may have occurred during context creation
776     unsigned long ssl_err = 0;
777     char ssl_err_str[256] = "no OpenSSL errors in queue";
778 
779     // Extract all errors from OpenSSL error queue
780     while ((ssl_err = ERR_get_error()) != 0) {
781       ERR_error_string_n(ssl_err, ssl_err_str, sizeof(ssl_err_str));
782       log_error("OpenSSL error: %lu: %s", ssl_err, ssl_err_str);
783     }
784 
785     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context (last OpenSSL error: %s)",
786                      ssl_err_str);
787   }
788 
789   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
790   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
791   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
792   if (!server->handler_pool) {
793     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
794     server->context = NULL;
795     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
796   }
797 
798   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
799   char ws_port_name[32];
800   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
801   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
802 
803   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
804   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
805 
806   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
807   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
808 
809   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
810   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
811 }

    websocket_server_config_t::client_handler,
    websocket_server::context, errno, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, ERROR_THREAD,
    websocket_server::handler, websocket_server::handler_pool,
    log_debug, log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    lws_log_init_server(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET,
    websocket_server::port, websocket_server_config_t::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO,
    thread_pool_create_with_workers(),
    websocket_server_config_t::tls_cert_path,
    websocket_server_config_t::tls_key_path,
    websocket_server::user_data, and
    websocket_server_config_t::user_data.


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run
  (websocket_server_t * server)


Run WebSocket server event loop. Blocks until server is signaled
    to stop via running flag.




Parameters


server Server instance


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 813 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


813                                                                    {
814   if (!server || !server->context) {
815     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
816   }
817 
818   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
819 
820   // Run libwebsockets event loop
821   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
822   int service_call_count = 0;
823   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
824     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
825     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
826     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
827     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
828     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
829     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
830     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
831       // > 30ms gap between service calls
832       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
833       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
834     }
835     service_call_count++;
836     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
837                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
838     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
839     if (result < 0) {
840       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
841       break;
842     }
843   }
844 
845   log_info(
846       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
847   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
848 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    websocket_server::context, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    log_debug_every, log_error, log_info,
    log_info_every, NAMED_DESCRIBE, websocket_server::port,
    websocket_server::running, SET_ERRNO, time_get_ns(),
    and US_PER_MS_INT.
